name: Zyloric / Allopurinol
source_note: Data compiled for Arogya knowledge base. Verify against 1mg.com and pharmeasy.in for live updates.
how_it_works: Xanthine oxidase inhibitor that blocks conversion of hypoxanthine and xanthine to uric acid, reducing serum urate levels.
food_interactions: Drink 2–3 liters of water daily — prevents kidney stones
Avoid high-purine foods (red meat, organ meats, shellfish, beer)
contraindications: Acute gout attack (wait for attack to resolve before starting)
HLA-B*5801 carriers (higher risk of severe reactions — test before use, especially in Asians)
what_to_avoid: Never start during an acute gout attack — wait 2–4 weeks after
Report rash immediately — can be fatal if ignored
Takes weeks to lower uric acid — be patient
